titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 588

p.seg: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to his sheriff of Berwick and all his worthy men of Lothian and of the Merse, greeting.

me to have conceded to [blank] Prior of Coldingham that he may remove his men of Coldinghamshire to inhabit the toun of Coldingham, whom in law he shall be able to remove. Wherefore I strictly forbid that anyone unlawfully hinder the aforesaid prior from removing all those whom in law he shall be able and is entitled to remove in order to inhabit the aforementioned toun.
As witnesses: Hugh, my chancellor; Richard de Prebenda, my clerk.
At Selkirk, on the 17th day of July.
